title: Trustworthy AI in Production: Building Reliable, Secure, and Observable AI Systems
speaker: Ravi Teja Reddy Mandala
summary: An engineering talk focusing on strategies and frameworks required for building reliable, secure, and highly observable AI systems in production environments.

title: Technical Dual-Session: Reliable AI Agents & Resilient Real-Time Systems
speaker: Vidisha Vijay & Ajay Pandey
summary: Session 1: Evaluation frameworks for tracking accuracy and robustness across multi-step LLM data pipelines. Session 2: Advanced engineering patterns and operational strategies for scaling infrastructure to handle heavy traffic and real-time event surges.

title: Why AI Agents Fail in Production – and How to Fix Them
speaker: Venkata Phani PatelKhana
summary: An engineering dive mapping out structural patterns for tracking production failures where runtime environments encounter dynamic real-world users. Highlights architectural system boundaries for building stable, transparent, and easy-to-debug applications.

title: ACM Richmond Official Launch Meeting
speaker: ACM Richmond Officers
summary: The formal foundational gathering for ACM Richmond. Introduced the chapter steering committee, laid out the organizational roadmap and mission, and provided a sneak-peek at upcoming technical presentation lineups.
